metadata.official_name: State University of New York at Binghamton

strategic_profile.executive_summary: Binghamton University is a premier public research university (R1) in the SUNY system, consistently recognized as a 'Public Ivy'. It offers highly rigorous academics, vibrant residential communities, and exceptional return on investment through robust career placement in the NYC metro area.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Out-of-state applicants have a 79% acceptance rate compared to a 31% in-state rate.
analyst_takeaways.implication: OOS students should aggressively target Binghamton as a high-value safety or target school.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Internal transfers (IUT) to Computer Science or the School of Management require a strict 3.5-3.7+ collegiate GPA.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Applicants must apply directly to their intended competitive major rather than trying to use Harpur College as a backdoor.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Median 10-year alumni earnings hit $80,304 against an in-state tuition of $7,070.
analyst_takeaways.implication: The university provides elite-level ROI, particularly for Business, Economics, and Engineering majors.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Collaborative but academically intense. Features an Oxford-style residential community model and heavy participation in intramurals and 450+ clubs.
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Suburban campus set on 930 acres in Vestal, NY, requiring a short drive or bus ride to downtown Binghamton nightlife and amenities.
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Exceptional undergraduate research access via the Freshman Research Immersion (FRI) and Summer Research Immersion (SRI) programs.

gpa.notes: Middle 50% reported as 93-98 on a 100-point scale.

colleges.name: Thomas J. Watson College of Engineering and Applied Science
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Highly quantitative students with strong backgrounds in calculus and physics seeking direct pipelines to defense and tech giants.

majors.name: Computer Science

core_courses: CS 101 Prof Skills Ethics & CS Trends
CS 220 Arch from a Prog Perspective

majors.notes: Internal transfer is strictly space-limited. Requires introductory CS course and writing composition course alongside math/science prereqs.

colleges.name: School of Management
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Ambitious pre-business students aiming for Big 4 accounting firms or Wall Street finance roles.

colleges.name: Harpur College of Arts and Sciences
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Students seeking a broad liberal arts foundation or those undecided on their major. Good for pre-med/pre-law.

supplemental_requirements.details: Requires completion of the Common App personal statement and SUNY Binghamton specific short answers.

major_selection_tactics: Apply directly to impacted programs like CS and Management rather than attempting internal transfers.
Leverage the out-of-state application pool, which accepts applicants at more than double the rate of the in-state pool.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: Applying to Harpur College is the easiest pathway into the university, but students must be comfortable graduating with a liberal arts degree, as transferring to Watson or SOM later is never guaranteed.

scholarships.name: Excelsior Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Covers base tuition for NY residents earning under $125k.

ap_policy.general_rule: Scores of 3, 4, or 5 receive credit. No credit for 1 or 2.
ap_policy.usage: Can fulfill major prerequisites, General Education requirements, or count as elective credits. Max 32 exam credits total.

ib_policy.general_rule: Credit awarded only for Higher Level (HL) exams with scores of 4-7.

transfer_articulation.restrictions: Students must complete at least 44 credits in residence at Harpur College (or 30 at Watson/40 at SOM) to earn a degree.

what_it_takes: Rigorous transcript with multiple AP/IB courses.
Strong demonstrated leadership and extracurricular involvement.
red_flags: Low GPA disguised by a heavy, unmanageable course load.
Assuming an easy internal transfer into CS or Business.
